champion.narrative: Champion is developing but not yet proven. Theresia Pardede (Head of Procurement, Decision Maker per Contact Role) is the sole active buyer contact and initiated the discovery call on 25 Jun 2026 with her extended Procurement team. She articulated clear pain (manual PR-to-PO conversion, Oracle eSourcing not adopted), stated requirements (eSourcing, eProcurement, Oracle EBS integration, Lark/Feishu integration, localization), and expressed AI interest ('AI inclined' per NextStep). She gated the demo on receiving pricing first, indicating she is driving the process internally. However, there is no evidence yet of champion behaviors: no introduction to the Economic Buyer, IT, or Finance; no coaching on decision process or competitive landscape; no internal advocacy or document sharing; no multi-threading facilitation. She is engaged and operational sponsor, but champion strength will be proven when she opens doors to power (Finance, C-level) and provides insider guidance. Current status: active operational sponsor, developing toward champion. Risk: single-threaded; if Theresia becomes unavailable or gets overruled by an unmapped Economic Buyer with different priorities, the deal goes dark.

competition.narrative: Competitive landscape is only partially understood. The primary 'competitor' is status quo: Oracle eSourcing exists in Traveloka's environment but is not activated (discovery call 25 Jun 2026), and current processes are manual (PR-to-PO conversion pain). This creates two risks: (1) Oracle reactivation—buyer could decide to invest in activating and training on the already-purchased Oracle module rather than buying Zycus ('it is already paid for' argument); (2) an undisclosed external vendor evaluation running in parallel (no competitor was named on the call, but absence of evidence is not evidence of absence—buyer may be benchmarking quietly). Theresia's question 'you want to try something more on agentic and more latest?' and stated 'AI inclined' suggest Zycus's AI/Merlin positioning is resonating and differentiating against Oracle's older architecture. However, the competitive set has not been surfaced. The wedge is clear: Zycus's agentic AI, modern UX, and Oracle EBS integration beat Oracle eSourcing's poor adoption and manual status quo—but this must be validated and positioned explicitly in the demo, and any parallel vendors must be smoked out by asking Theresia directly during the pricing/demo conversation.

Merlin Intake and AI sourcing should be the centerpiece of the demo to capitalize on this appetite and differentiate vs Oracle.
